mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% after the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion, which compresses input cost margins and boosts equity risk premiums, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates typically pressure equity valuations, but if housing starts remain above 1.4 million units, resilient construction demand offsets rate sensitivity, resulting in neutral-to-positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn decisively negative when FEDFUNDS rises and HOUST remains below 1.4 million, contradicting the offset mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust aggregate demand and corporate hiring expansion, which supports earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 across all 10-year rolling windows, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, the economy enters a high-growth regime that expands corporate profit margins and reduces recession probabilities, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%, indicating the threshold does not capture earnings acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment and stable federal funds rates indicates a healthy labor market operating without monetary over-tightening, which sustains consumer spending power and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining UNRATE and flat FEDFUNDS fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term loses predictive power after controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising industrial production typically signals supply chain normalization rather than demand overheating, which eases margin compression and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL inflation above 3.0%, indicating that supply growth is being priced as demand-pull inflation rather than margin relief.
